effort_complexity.rationale: Small code surface, but the semantics thread through the just-rewired loop state machine; retry/escalation interaction is the risk.

design_decisions.choice: D1 — blocked signal = the EXISTING driver.Status enum member StatusBlocked plus one additive Result.BlockedReason string (zero-value default = today's semantics); no separate Blocked bool; no driver emits StatusBlocked this slice (spec out_of_scope item 4).
options: Existing StatusBlocked enum member + additive BlockedReason string (chosen) — one source of truth for blockedness on the Result struct; spec in_scope item 1 explicitly allows 'blocked boolean OR verdict enum member'; matches the ErrKindAuth-style vocab-binding precedent and the S01 contract-file additive-change discipline (R-02 mitigation).
A separate Blocked bool field alongside Status (rejected) — two sources of truth for one fact on the same struct; a driver could emit Blocked=true with Status=ok.
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach) acknowledged proceed-as-designed: captain-proceed.md@31c3290 pin 3 ('D1 confirmed — vocab-binding precedent applies; enum + additive BlockedReason, zero-value default, no driver emission this slice — proceed exactly as designed'), ratifying Captain review.md pin 3 (memory-cited: project_driver_contract_recut), design commit 051de7b.
design_decisions.rationale: Touches the S01 contract file all four drivers implement, but zero-value semantics keep every driver byte-identical; the runner keys ONLY off Status and never infers blockedness from prose — the exact anti-pattern this slice removes.

design_decisions.choice: D2 — the shared blocked-lane sentinel constant lives in internal/orchestrator (BlockedLaneSentinel), value identical to run's private errVerdictBlockedPrefix string; run aliases its prefix to the shared constant.
options: internal/orchestrator (chosen) — scheduler cannot import run (run imports scheduler); both already import orchestrator; precedent: orchestrator.InterpreterInconclusiveSentinel consumed the same way in worker.go.
Duplicate the string literal in scheduler (rejected — drift risk).
internal/verdict (rejected — it is a RunSlice error-shape fact, not verdict vocabulary).
design_decisions.human_decision: Type-2 noted default; acknowledged in captain-proceed.md@31c3290 (pin 4 catch-all accepting the Captain's suggested reply, which acknowledged D2-D6).
design_decisions.rationale: One constant, two consumers; the string value is unchanged so run.IsBlocked and every existing assertion hold.

design_decisions.choice: D3 — no new scheduler TrackResult enum value: TrackFail remains the carrier for blocked lanes; the blocked/failed distinction travels ONLY via the new WorkerOptions.RecordBlocked side-channel, which the RunParallel exit report consumes. Persisted supervisor state for a blocked lane is supervisor.StateFailed (NEVER the string 'blocked', which supervisor.Release silently coerces to 'done' — supervisor.go:205-207).
options: TrackFail + RecordBlocked side-channel + releaseTrack(supervisor.StateFailed) (chosen) — TestRunTrack_TerminalDriverErrorHaltsTrack (S07 AC-03) asserts TrackFail on exactly this error shape; TrackBlocked is already taken by invariant-2; failCancel() dependent-phase skip inherited for free; no AC requires a distinct scheduler enum (AC-05 requires a distinct REPORT).
New TrackBlockedLane TrackResult = 'blocked_lane' (rejected) — forces edits to existing scheduler tests and supervisor-state plumbing for zero AC value, violating the AC-06 gate.

releaseTrack('blocked') (rejected per Captain review pin 1, CRITICAL) — supervisor.Release coerces unknown states to StateDone, recording a blocked (unmergeable, replan-required) lane as complete in the tracks DB.
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach), captain-proceed.md@31c3290 pin 1 (CRITICAL, accepted): use supervisor.StateFailed as the persisted supervisor state; do NOT pass a string Release silently rewrites; the blocked-vs-failed distinction travels via RecordBlocked. D3's rejected TrackBlockedLane alternative explicitly acknowledged and agreed in the Captain's reply (§2).
design_decisions.rationale: The load-bearing decision: keeps every existing retry/terminal scheduler test green unmodified (AC-06) while giving the report the distinction it needs.

design_decisions.choice: D4 — implement.Run early-returns (nil error, no state transition) on Result.Status==StatusBlocked: the slice stays in_progress; RunSlice owns the terminal blocked handling (status write, commit, notify, sentinel error).
options: Early return in implement.Run, terminal handling in RunSlice (chosen) — keeps implement.Run's 'never certifies' contract and exactly one blocked-handling site; also fixes the latent bug where a blocked dispatch fell through to proof generation and a spurious implemented transition.
Handle blocked entirely inside implement.Run (rejected — splits terminal status/commit/notify ownership across two packages).
design_decisions.human_decision: Type-2 noted default; acknowledged in captain-proceed.md@31c3290 (pin 4 catch-all).
design_decisions.rationale: Board/router resume path verified sound by Captain review pin 4: oracle derives Blocked from verification.result regardless of state (board/oracle.go:233); router checks ss.Blocked before the state switch and routes /replan-release (router.go:100-110).

design_decisions.choice: D5 — the blocked-vs-fail exit report is embedded in RunParallel's returned error plus the loop log; no new printing surface; byte-identical legacy error format when no blocked lane exists. TUI/board surfacing stays out of scope.
options: Report in the returned error + loop log (chosen) — cmd/sworn/run.go already prints the returned error and exits non-zero; protects TestRunParallel_FailureCascade and unknown callers matching the legacy format.
A new report printer / TUI surface (rejected — spec out_of_scope item 3).
design_decisions.human_decision: Type-2 noted default; acknowledged in captain-proceed.md@31c3290 (pin 4 catch-all).
design_decisions.rationale: AC-05 requires a distinguishing report on a non-zero exit, not a presentation surface.

design_decisions.choice: D6 — blocked lanes skip circuit-breaker fingerprinting and the worker-level track_failed notification (RunSlice already emitted the blocked notification); terminal auth/credits driver halts JOIN the BLOCKED report section as designed, with NO reason-string sniffing to carve them out.
options: Skip breaker + skip duplicate notify; accept the terminal-driver-error widening (chosen) — a blocked lane must not accrue breaker pages; removes an existing double-notify on verifier-blocked lanes; auth/credits halts are blocked-class by nature (re-dispatch cannot help) and their verbatim reason self-explains the credentials remedy.
Carve terminal driver errors out of the BLOCKED section by matching their reason text (rejected per captain-proceed.md pin 3 — reason-string sniffing is the exact anti-pattern this slice removes).
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach), captain-proceed.md@31c3290 pin 3: terminal driver errors join the BLOCKED report section, accepted as designed; no reason-string sniffing.
design_decisions.rationale: Captain review flag (c) verified regression-safety: no scheduler test wires a Notifier or asserts track_failed; the notify-skip and breaker-skip cannot break the protected set.

reachability_artifacts: internal/run/blocked_terminal_test.go:TestLoopBlockedImplementerTerminal (fired S05 replay through the real run.RunSlice entry point)
internal/run/blocked_report_test.go:TestLoopExitReportBlockedVsFail (real run.RunParallel exit report, BLOCKED vs FAIL lanes)
